records.ety: [L. contributus, p. p. of contribuere to bring together, to add; con- + tribuere to grant, impart. See Tribute.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Contributed; p. pr. & vb. n. Contributing.]

defs: To give or grant i common with others; to give to a common stock or for a common purpose; to furnish or supply in part; to give (money or other aid) for a specified object; as, to contribute food or fuel for the poor.
quotes: England contributes much more than any other of the allies.

defs: To give a part to a common stock; to lend assistance or aid, or give something, to a common purpose; to have a share in any act or effect.
quotes: We are engaged in war; the secretary of state calls upon the colonies to contribute.

defs: To give or use one's power or influence for any object; to assist.
quotes: These men also contributed to obstruct the progress of wisdom.
